summary: Independent read-only QA rejects M3-POSTWAR-GOVERNANCE-001 after finding an unclosed same-district stale-rights variant. A restored-vassal postwar arrangement by victor 1 can be followed by direct-control for the same district by victor 3; the second command is accepted, district controller becomes 3, but local polity 2 remains suzerain-linked to victor 1 with active tribute/troop obligations to victor 1. Existing validation reports zero invariant errors.

findings.reproduction: Inline Node repro: create a 3-polity M3 world; apply restore-vassal-ruler for victorPolityId=1/localPolityId=2/districtId=2; then apply direct-control for victorPolityId=3/localPolityId=2/districtId=2 with a different settlementId.
findings.observed: firstStatus=accepted, secondStatus=accepted, districtController=3, localSuzerain=1, active obligations remain debtor=2 creditor=1 for tribute and troop, validateWorldStateV0(second.runtime.world)=[]
findings.impact: Violates no illegal overlapping rights and coherent same-district postwar arrangement semantics. The district has one controller, but old suzerain and obligations survive under a different victor, so the authority/obligation state is internally misleading and not caught by tests or invariants.
findings.necessary_fix: Make the postwar arrangement guard district-scoped for existing postwar arrangements regardless of victor, or implement an explicit replacement command that deterministically retires/clears prior suzerain, obligations, and enfeoffment rights with events. Add regression coverage for different-victor same-district restore-vassal to direct-control and/or tribute-only attempts.

r1_r2_assessment.reason: R2 tests cover same-victor same-district duplicate settlementId changes and direct-after-vassal through the fixed helper victor, but do not cover a different victor applying a second same-district arrangement.

scope_assessment.scope_note: Branch diff plus current status paths are all under allowed_paths and none match forbidden_paths.
scope_assessment.scope_creep: No M4 campaign/marching/battle/siege/supply/reinforcement, full diplomacy/treaty/legitimacy/rebellion, client UI, content-source, save-format migration, historical content, or new dependency implementation drift found.

tests_run.result: status REVIEW, route_to systems_architect, risk R3
no matches in changed implementation/test surfaces
matches only in task/handoff negative-scope text and pre-existing save-format import; no diff drift
architecture passed; root 24 files/154 tests; web 1 file/3 tests

blockers: Reject or explicitly replace any existing same-district postwar arrangement across different victor polities.
Add regression tests proving the different-victor same-district path cannot leave stale suzerain, enfeoffment, or obligation rights.
decisions: REQUEST_CHANGES: R1 same-victor findings are fixed, but same-district different-victor replacement still leaves stale suzerain and obligation rights.
REQUEST_CHANGES: systems R2 ACCEPT is not sufficient until different-victor same-district postwar arrangements are rejected or explicitly replaced.
risks: If same-district different-victor arrangements remain accepted, authority and obligation state can diverge while validateWorldStateV0 reports no invariant errors.
Replacement semantics are intentionally out of scope unless implemented with deterministic retirement/clearing events and separate review.
requested_action: Repair the same-district different-victor stale-rights path, add focused regression coverage, rerun required gates, and return to systems_architect/qa review.
